mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are not statistically higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment rate exceeding 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales growth accelerates while housing starts decelerate indicates a shift from durable goods to services consumption, signaling economic fragility that suppresses equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or forward returns do not decline when the divergence occurs.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion, rapid industrial production growth outpaces demand absorption, leading to inventory buildup and margin compression, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive during periods of rapid industrial production growth in late-cycle regimes, or Rank IC is negative over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 3.0%, inflationary pressures force expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which support higher equity valuations and produce positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous high real GDP growth and elevated inflation indicate an overheating economy where monetary policy will likely tighten, creating a negative risk premium that drives down S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint effect of high GDP and high inflation does not predict negative returns, or the interaction term is insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a decline in industrial production signals that higher borrowing costs are successfully cooling the economy, which reduces recession fears and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when industrial production falls during tightening periods, or the interaction fails to predict returns in walk-forward validation.
